title: Our Changing Earth
passage:
Our Changing Earth

Our Earth is getting warmer. This warming is called global warming. For many years, people used coal, oil, and gas to make electricity and run cars. These things make the air dirty. This dirty air traps heat from the sun like a greenhouse. Because of this, the temperature of the planet is going up every year.

Global warming changes our weather in many ways. In some places, the summer is very hot and there is no rain. This makes the land dry. In other places, there is too much rain. This causes big floods that damage homes and roads. Also, the ice in cold places like the Arctic is melting fast. When the ice melts, it turns into water. This water goes into the ocean, and the sea level rises. This is dangerous for people living near the beach.

Animals also have problems because of climate change. Polar bears live on the ice. When the ice melts, they cannot find food easily. Many birds and fish are also moving to colder places because their old homes are too warm now.

But we can help our Earth. First, we can save energy. We should turn off the lights when we leave a room. Second, we can walk or ride bicycles instead of driving cars. Third, we can plant more trees. Trees are very good because they clean the air. If we all work together, we can keep our planet clean and safe.

questions.text: What is the warming of the Earth called?
questions.explanation: The passage states: "This warming is called global warming."

questions.explanation: The passage states: "When the ice melts, it turns into water. This water goes into the ocean, and the sea level rises."

questions.text: People used coal, oil, and gas to make electricity.
questions.explanation: The passage states: "For many years, people used coal, oil, and gas to make electricity..."

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.explanation: The passage states that hot summers with no rain make the land dry, while too much rain causes big floods. Floods do not dry the land.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.text: Polar bears find it easy to get food when ice melts.
questions.explanation: The passage states: "When the ice melts, they cannot find food easily."

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that "we can plant more trees" because they clean the air, but there is no mention of the cost or if it is expensive.

questions.explanation: The passage states: "Polar bears live on the ice."

vocabulary.meaning: keeps something in one place and does not let it escape (giữ lại, bẫy lại)
large amounts of water covering an area that is usually dry (lũ lụt)
changing from solid ice into liquid water (tan chảy)
power used to make things work, like electricity (năng lượng)

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (Tactics)

1. Kỹ thuật tìm từ khóa (Scanning): Với trình độ A1-A2, các từ khóa trong câu hỏi xuất hiện gần như giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy khoanh vùng các từ nổi bật như "polar bears", "bicycles", "lights", "traps heat" để tìm nhanh đoạn văn chứa câu trả lời.

2. True/False/Not Given: Đối với câu hỏi Not Given, nếu bạn không tìm thấy bất kỳ từ ngữ nào nói về "giá cả" hay "đắt đỏ" (expensive) của việc trồng cây, hãy tự tin chọn NOT GIVEN.

3. Giới hạn từ (Word Limit): Hãy chú ý yêu cầu "NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS". Ví dụ, câu 10 chỉ cần điền "bicycles" hoặc "ride bicycles" là đủ, tránh viết quá số từ quy định.
